audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Timothy text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for sincere trust, gift rekindled, power/love/soundness, suffering, deposit guarded.

verses.restored: Paul, apostle of Messiah Jesus through the will of the Divine, according to promise of life in Messiah Jesus,
to Timothy, beloved child: grace, mercy, peace from Divine Father and Messiah Jesus our Lord.
I have gratitude to the Divine, whom I serve from ancestors in a pure conscience, as I have unceasing remembrance of you in my petitions night and day,
longing to see you, remembering your tears, so that I may be filled with joy,
having received reminder of the unhypocritical trust in you, which dwelt first in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice, and I have been persuaded that it is also in you.
For this reason I remind you to rekindle the grace-gift of the Divine that is in you through the laying on of my hands.
For the Divine did not give us a spirit of fear/cowardice, but of power and love and soundness of mind.
Therefore do not be ashamed of the witness of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner, but share hardship for the good news according to the power of the Divine,
the One saving/healing us and calling us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, given to us in Messiah Jesus before age-long times,
but now made manifest through the appearing of our Savior/Healer Messiah Jesus, who made death inactive and brought life and incorruptibility to light through the good news,
for which I was appointed herald and apostle and teacher.
For this reason I also suffer these things; but I am not ashamed, for I know whom I have trusted and have been persuaded that he is able to guard my deposit for that day.
Hold the pattern of healthy words that you heard from me, in trust and love that are in Messiah Jesus.
Guard the beautiful/good deposit through holy Spirit dwelling in us.
You know this, that all those in Asia turned away from me, among whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes.
May the Lord give mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, because often he refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chain,
but when in Rome he eagerly searched for me and found me.
May the Lord give him to find mercy from the Lord in that day; and how many services he rendered in Ephesus, you know very well.

translation_notes: The central gift language is rekindling, not striving for status.
Spirit is power, love, and soundness rather than fear/cowardice.
Guarding the deposit is communal and Spirit-enabled, not control-based.
